title: Spooky Pooky's Handmade Gifts
summary: When Spooky Pooky the pig finds his favorite bookcase broken, his mother Mama Pooky steps in to fix it. Grateful for her help, Spooky Pooky crafts a special brown and white bead necklace for her. Later, the pair realizes they have forgotten a gift for their friend Raccoon's birthday party. They quickly return home to handmade beautiful pink paper lanterns. The story concludes with a joyful celebration where the Pooky family shares their handmade gifts, sings Happy Birthday, and enjoys cake with all their friends.

highlightQuote.text: The problem was his bookcase was broken and it had a hole in it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh look, how's that?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I see some stars.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's tell stories! Sit in the chair!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Sit in the chair with us. Tell me stories with us. Tell me stories with us.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay cookie, let's make stories together.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'll sit in your lap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Sit sit. Would you like your flashlight? You're sitting in cookie's lap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, here's your flashlight. Okay, let me hold you.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: All right. Now we've got stars, so we can make up some stories.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, and remember, if you have to go potty just let me know and we can go to the bathroom and use the potty.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Why, Dad? Why?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Why? Because you put your pee-pee in the potty, that's where it goes.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Come on.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay. Once upon a time, there was a very creative and smart and kind pig named...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Spooky Pooky!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Named Spooky Pooky. But on this day, Spooky Pooky had a problem. What was the problem?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: The problem was his bookcase was broken and it had a hole in it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: His bookcase was broken and had a hole in it.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And his mama was fixed it. His mama was Spooky Pooky.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Spooky Pooky's mama was also Spooky Pooky? Yeah? How about we call her Mama Pooky?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Mama Pooky.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, Mama Pooky came to fix the bookcase. And how did she fix it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She fixed it by putting the screw in it and she screwed it.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And she screwed the bookcase back together, and that fixed the problem. Was Spooky Pooky happy?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So what did Spooky Pooky do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He read some books.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He read some books. And then he thought, hmm, maybe I'll do something nice to thank Mama Pooky for fixing my bookcase. And so what did he do?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He try making a necklace for her.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He tried making a necklace for her. What did he make the necklace out of?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Beads.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Beads. Oh, what color were the beads?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They were brown and a little bit white.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Brown and a little bit white. And did he make a pattern out of the beads?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. They were round ones.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: They were round ones. Oh. And then did he give the necklace to his mama? Yeah. And then she wore it and she said, oh thank you Spooky Pooky, I love this necklace. I'm going to wear it out tonight because tonight is a very special event. What was the special event that night?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: She wore it all the night.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, Mama Pooky wore it all night because she was going out for someone's birthday party. Whose birthday was it?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was Raccoon's.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Raccoon's birthday! And so later that day, it was time to go to Raccoon's house for the birthday party. And Mama Pooky and Spooky Pooky got in the car to go, and then Mama Pooky said, oh no, we don't have a gift for Raccoon.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: We can go back home and make one.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Make one! What did they make as a gift for Raccoon?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe it was some lanterns.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Some lanterns. That's a very nice gift idea for Raccoon. Raccoon loves lanterns. What kind of lanterns did they make? What did they make them out of?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Paper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, paper lanterns. And did they decorate them? And how did they decorate them?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Yes. They decorated them with glue and paper.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, and were they different colors? What colors were they?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They were both pink.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Both lanterns were pink. That sounds like a very nice gift. And so Mama Pooky and Spooky Pooky made the pink lanterns, and they got back in the car and they drove to Raccoon's party. And who did they see when they got there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Raccoon!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And they said happy birthday Raccoon! And who else was there?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I don't know.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Mama Pooky was very excited to show everyone the beautiful necklace of brown and white beads that Spooky Pooky had made for her and that she was wearing to the party. And they said hello friends, hello everyone. And they said wow what a beautiful necklace.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: They were lanterns.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, Mama Pooky was wearing the necklace that Spooky Pooky made for her earlier in the day, but their gift for Raccoon was the lanterns, that's right. And they brought the gift for Raccoon, and everyone else brought their gifts too. And then they sang to the Raccoon because it was her birthday. So what did they sing?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Happy birthday, happy birthday to you, happy birthday to you!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yay! And everyone clapped and then they ate the birthday cake. And Raccoon opened all her presents, including the beautiful pink lanterns from the Pooky family.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And you put them up. Ah ah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, put them up. The end.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And then you put them down and put them together and clap! Clap clap clap! Try clapping all by yourself, how about that?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Hi. Hi Izzy, I love you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'll sit in your lap.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, thank you, I love for you to sit in my lap. That's so cozy.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Let's see what's in here.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, those are just going to go in the bins. We can close that. We don't need to take them out right now.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Could we read a book?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Yeah, we could read a book. Okay baby, since we're getting ready for bed, it's time to take off your necklaces. Thank you.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Can I get up here?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh look at you, you can get up on the chair. Time to turn off the stars and turn on our lamp. Wait a minute, where did he go?

characters.name: Spooky Pooky
characters.description: A creative and kind pig who enjoys reading and making gifts for his family and friends.

characters.name: Mama Pooky
characters.description: Spooky Pooky's mother who fixes furniture and wears the handmade jewelry her son creates.

characters.name: Raccoon
characters.description: A friend of the Pooky family who loves lanterns and is celebrated at a large birthday party.

places.name: The Pooky Home
places.description: Where Spooky Pooky lives, reads books, and crafts handmade gifts with his mother.

places.name: Raccoon's House
places.description: The site of a festive birthday party where friends gather to celebrate with cake and presents.
